DEVIATION REPORT
Deviation Number: DEV-2025-0089
Date Initiated: 2025-04-03
Initiated By: Michael Torres, Manufacturing Operator
Supervisor: Lisa Park, Production Supervisor
Classification: Major
Status: Under Investigation

1. DESCRIPTION OF DEVIATION

On April 3, 2025 at approximately 14:30, during routine inspection of the cold storage room (Room 210-B), Manufacturing Operator M. Torres observed that the temperature logger was displaying a reading of 12°C. The specified storage temperature for Room 210-B is 2°C–8°C.

Review of the continuous temperature log indicates that the room temperature exceeded 8°C beginning at approximately 08:15 on April 3, 2025 and remained out of range until 16:45 when the HVAC issue was corrected. Total duration of excursion: approximately 8.5 hours.

Products potentially affected: Drug Substance DS-API-2025-001 (100 kg), Drug Substance DS-API-2025-002 (75 kg), finished product intermediate FP-INT-2025-008.

Note: Temperature data was recorded in the log by the operator after the shift ended, not at the time of observation.

2. IMMEDIATE ACTIONS TAKEN

2.1 The affected materials were immediately quarantined and labeled "On Hold — Deviation Under Investigation."
2.2 Facilities Engineering was notified and the HVAC malfunction was diagnosed as a failed compressor valve.
2.3 HVAC repair was completed by 16:45 on April 3, 2025; temperature returned to 4°C.
2.4 QA Manager was notified at 17:00 on April 3, 2025.

3. IMPACT ASSESSMENT

3.1 Drug substances stored in Room 210-B during the excursion may have been affected depending on their temperature sensitivity profiles.
3.2 Affected materials are on hold pending stability and quality impact assessment.
3.3 Product integrity has been potentially compromised.
3.4 Distribution status: No affected product has been distributed.

4. ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS

4.1 Immediate Cause
HVAC compressor valve failed, causing temperature to rise above specification.

4.2 Root Cause
The root cause is equipment failure due to inadequate preventive maintenance. The compressor valve was at end of its service life.

4.3 Contributing Factors
Equipment age.

5. CORRECTIVE AND PREVENTIVE ACTIONS

5.1 CAPA-2025-0052 has been initiated to address the root cause.
5.2 Planned corrective actions include:
   CA-1: Replace failed compressor valve (Complete — April 3, 2025)
   CA-2: Review PM schedule for all HVAC equipment in cold storage areas (Due: April 17, 2025)
5.3 Preventive actions:
   PA-1: Implement redundant temperature alarm system with automatic notification (Due: June 1, 2025)
   PA-2: Increase PM frequency for critical HVAC equipment (Due: May 1, 2025)

6. REGULATORY CONSIDERATION

No regulatory reporting assessment has been documented at this time.

7. QUALITY RISK ASSESSMENT

Stability testing of retained samples from the affected drug substances will be conducted to determine if product quality is maintained within acceptable limits. Results expected within 30 days.

8. ATTACHMENTS

A. Temperature log printout (Room 210-B, April 3, 2025)
B. HVAC repair work order
C. Material inventory in Room 210-B at time of excursion

Signatures:
Initiator: Michael Torres _________________ Date: April 4, 2025
QA Review: _________________ Date: _________
QA Approval: _________________ Date: _________
